key_points: Off-campus Varsity apartments: unauthorized entry, hostile staff, double-charging
Dorms had no heat or hot water during 15°F winter storm
E-scooter ban triggered by dorm charging fires; campus too big to walk
key_quotes: They used my roommate's SPOON to pry open the drain
It's so weird that lots of money goes to sports, while student facilities remain shit
Shuttle UM is so damn unreliable. The campus is too big for that

key_points: Active campus culture: lightsaber battles, club events, strong community feel
Some seniors regret staying isolated; Greek life present but not dominant
Political provocateurs (Turning Point USA) regularly set up on campus
key_quotes: I focused on grades and kept my head down. Now that it's almost over, it feels like I missed out
Don't feed their ego!
May the 4th be with y'all crazy kids

key_points: South dining hall ran above 90°F for weeks; plates hot enough to cause blisters
Food quality described as inconsistent and bland at South
Board and Brew café had a health code violation; campus food safety spotty
key_quotes: South is literally unsafe to eat at right now
Food is so inconsistent and bland. Sorry bout it
and turf is still open [after Board and Brew violation]

key_points: Students openly discuss burnout, isolation, and feeling like they wasted college
Professor disability accommodation violations reported multiple times; systemic issue
Sick students pressured to attend class by professors who won't allow makeups
key_quotes: I woke up, drove to school, came home, did nothing, rinse and repeat
Under no circumstances should you go to school when you're sick and potentially contagious. Wtf
UMD needs to stop looking the other way when they learn about these situations

key_points: Maryland state budget shortfall of $3B is cutting university funding
Over $30 million in federal grants canceled due to Trump administration cuts
Merit scholarships exist but are modest compared to peer schools
key_quotes: About $30 million worth of UMD grants have been canceled
University community members say proposed USM budget cuts could harm students
I got into University of Maryland CS, with a $60k scholarship! It's actually the smallest of the three packages I've received

key_points: CS ranked #16 nationally; legitimate strong program with beloved professors retiring
Widespread AI/ChatGPT cheating in classes, including during finals and by TAs grading code
Admitted to Letters & Sciences (undeclared) by default if not directly into major
key_quotes: For my CS finals, I saw at least 5 people pull out GPT on their phone during the exam
80% of the time I plug your writing into an AI detector it flags the whole thing
Wdym Letters and Sciences?? I didn't grind for 4 years to be academically undecided-core

key_points: Slow and opaque response to crises: lice infestation, cyberattack, no heat in dorms
Career Center sent students AI-generated slop emails
DEI office quietly renamed under political pressure; admissions lost a student's transcript
key_quotes: Beyond disgusting behavior if true — the university should have informed students immediately!
I'd be pissed. Your sole job is helping students with this and you need AI help?
'It is not their responsibility to check every student's individual checklist' — UMD Admissions

key_points: Purple Line light rail finally arriving after 15+ years of delays; major convenience win
Campus is visually beautiful, especially in fall and snow, but physically large
College Park area has safety concerns; recurring break-in warnings near campus
key_quotes: When I was at UMD they told me it would be finished by 2022 and now it's 2026
I always get a little homesick for UMD this time of year
Every year, sometimes more than once, creepy men attempt to break into houses near campus

key_points: Strong DC/federal job proximity; CS and engineering grads well-positioned
Sergey Brin (Google co-founder) is the most famous UMD undergrad alumni
COMM major reported applying to hundreds of jobs post-grad with no success
key_quotes: UMCP is highly ranked, but am skeptical of its location — more federal jobs than IT
I graduated as a COMM major and have had trouble finding a job/internship
Another interesting thing: many who went to a non-T20 school go on to a T20 grad school

key_points: Strong value for Maryland in-state students, especially in CS and engineering
Out-of-state tuition harder to justify given peer competition
State budget cuts may mean fewer resources with tuition increases ahead
key_quotes: I'm having a hard time justifying it financially, what with UF's in-state tuition
It would be nice if Maryland protected its students like TX does
My parents make 250k and they're telling me they can't pay for my school

key_points: Large, spirited public university with genuine community pride and quirky traditions
Academic intensity varies wildly by major; CS/engineering grind, other majors coast
Campus culture is politically engaged, sometimes chaotic, rarely boring
key_quotes: I don't like the academic atmosphere here. People don't care about academics
lmao UMD's finest
I used to pray for times like this

key_points: Infrastructure: no heat or hot water in dorms during sub-freezing weather storm
Health & Safety: confirmed body lice infestation in McKeldin Library with delayed university response
Security: campus cyberattack during finals week knocked out ELMS access; phishing page harvested student logins
key_quotes: It's cold asf and I can't sleep cuz I'm freezing my ass off
The university should have informed students immediately!
12k is gone in my bank account. I really have no idea what to do

key_points: Legendary CS professors like Fawzi Emad make difficult courses genuinely memorable
Testudo statue and campus traditions create real school identity and community
Proximity to DC opens government, research, and internship pipelines unavailable elsewhere
key_quotes: One of my favorite professors from my time in UMD CS. His projects were the coolest
I always get a little homesick for UMD this time of year
Probably the highest profile athlete to ever pick Maryland in any sport
